differentials.name: BENIGN TUMORS < 1% of all clinical esophageal tumors; 20% at autopsy (a)  Subepithelial tumor (75%) =  nonepithelial, intramural
differentials.raw: BENIGN TUMORS < 1% of all clinical esophageal tumors; 20% at autopsy (a)  Subepithelial tumor (75%) =  nonepithelial, intramural

differentials.name: Leiomyoma (50% of all benign tumors) ◊  Most common subepithelial mass in esophagus
differentials.raw: Leiomyoma (50% of all benign tumors) ◊  Most common subepithelial mass in esophagus

differentials.name: Squamous papilloma =  most common benign mucosal tumor; rarely multiple
differentials.raw: Squamous papilloma =  most common benign mucosal tumor; rarely multiple (as in esophageal papillomatosis)

differentials.name: Inflammatory esophagogastric polyp =  sentinel polyp = bulbous tip of thickened gastric fold
differentials.raw: Inflammatory esophagogastric polyp =  sentinel polyp = bulbous tip of thickened gastric fold

context: √  primary wave stops at level of tumor √  proximal esophageal dilatation + hypotonicity √  rigid esophageal wall at site of tumoral implant √  disorganized / altered / effaced mucosal folds around defect √  tumor shadow on tangential view extending beyond esophageal margin (b)  Mucosal tumor (25%) = epithelial, intraluminal √  small sessile slightly lobulated polyp √  average size of 4 mm
